description: All drones except Nano category (under 250g) must be registered on the DigitalSky platform and obtain a Unique Identification Number (UIN). Registration requires submitting drone specifications, owner details, and purpose of use.

Remote Pilot Licence is mandatory for operating drones above Micro category (2kg). Applicants must be 18+, pass written and practical exams, and complete training from DGCA-approved institute. Valid for 10 years.
penalty_description: Fine up to INR 50000 plus potential imprisonment

description: All drones must have NPNT firmware — any flight requires digital unlock via DigitalSky. Drone automatically cannot arm without approved flight plan. Ensures enforcement of airspace restrictions.

Green zones are areas below 400 feet AGL that are not in restricted/prohibited airspace and not within airport approach/departure paths. Operations in green zones for Micro and Nano do not require flight plan approval.
penalty_description: No penalty for compliant operations in green zones

description: Yellow zones require ATC clearance before operations. These include areas within 12 km of airport perimeters, near helipads, and designated sensitive areas. Flight plan must be submitted via DigitalSky.
penalty_description: Heavy fine and possible criminal charges for unauthorized yellow zone flight

description: Red zones are strictly prohibited areas: near international borders (25km), within 5km of Rashtrapati Bhavan, over military installations, nuclear facilities, and eco-sensitive zones. No drone operations permitted under any circumstance.
Third-party insurance is mandatory for all drones except Nano category. Insurance must cover damage to third parties and property during drone operations.
Type Certificate from Quality Council of India is required for all drones manufactured or imported for use in India. Certifies the drone meets safety and airworthiness standards.
penalty_description: Import or commercial use without Type Certificate is punishable

description: All drone operations must remain below 400 feet Above Ground Level (AGL). Higher altitude operations require special approval from DGCA and coordination with ATC.
penalty_description: Fine and potential criminal prosecution for breach of altitude limits

description: Any drone operation for hire or reward requires a Commercial Operating Permit in addition to RPL and registration. Must submit operational manual and demonstrate safety procedures.
penalty_description: Fine up to INR 1 lakh for unauthorized commercial operations

description: Drone operations during night time (30 min after sunset to 30 min before sunrise) require special endorsement on RPL and additional equipment including anti-collision lights visible from 3km.
Nano: up to 250g. Micro: 250g-2kg. Small: 2kg-25kg. Medium: 25kg-150kg. Large: above 150kg. Each category has different regulatory requirements for registration, licensing, and operation.
Drone import requires approval from DGCA. An import clearance certificate must be obtained before customs release. Import of drones without approval is treated as smuggling.

Drone operators must not capture images or data that violates individual privacy. Operations near residential areas must maintain minimum distance. Data collected must be stored securely per IT Act 2000.
penalty_description: Penalty under IT Act and possible drone confiscation

description: NDMA, SDMA, and authorized agencies may operate drones without standard approvals during declared emergencies and disaster relief operations. Must still ensure safety of manned aviation.
penalty_description: Exemption - no penalty for authorized agencies
